hypothesis: A single confidence threshold can satisfy a population-average accuracy tolerance and save cost while repeatedly violating a preregistered accuracy tolerance in a rare, miscalibrated high-stakes stratum.
claimBoundary: The saved experiment demonstrates a case-mix and calibration failure mechanism under declared synthetic Bernoulli outcomes; it does not measure a vendor model, select universal thresholds, or prove that stratum-aware routing transfers without prospective calibration.

baselines: oracle router that observes which model would be correct
controls: calibrated-score control removes the critical confidence shift
equal-skill control removes the small-versus-strong accuracy gap
paired request stream shared by all routing policies
